AI Funding Radar · Weekly
This Week in Grants
June 8, 2026  ·  435 real-money grants  ·  16 credit programs (later)
For AI Product Teams
Compute credits, accelerators, and startup funding for small AI product teams.  ·  142 new
Stacks Ecosystem Grants Program 💵 CASH  VIABLE
Stacks Foundation / Stacks Endowment  •  Builder Grants up to $50,000; Getting Started Grants up to $10,000 (paid in sBTC/STStacks)  •  Rolling (program live under 2026 Treasury budget)
Re-launched in 2026 under the Stacks Endowment (post SIP-031), with Builder Grants (generally up to $50K) and Getting Started Grants (up to $10K) now open on a rolling basis for tooling, apps, and infrastructure on the Bitcoin L2.
VIABLE: Non-dilutive cash, no equity, simple proposal form. Requires a Stacks-relevant build angle — frame a Castalia/Vox component as Bitcoin-L2 tooling or onchain coordination infra.
web3 · public-goods · bitcoin · rolling · non-dilutive   Open program →
Mantle Grants Program (Public Grants) 💵 CASH  VIABLE
Mantle Network  •  Public Grants ~$20,000 in MNT; larger Scouts / EcoFund tracks ($200M pool)  •  Rolling (Buildathons and grants run continuously through the year)
Mantle runs continuous Public Grants (~$20K MNT) for early-stage projects, plus a Scouts program (Season 2 targeting AI/consumer/payments apps) and a $200M EcoFund. Rolling buildathons feed top teams into additional grant support.
VIABLE: Cash in MNT, no equity, rolling. Season 2 explicitly wants AI applications — strong fit for an AI-integrated collaboration product if deployed/integrated with Mantle.
web3 · AI · rolling · non-dilutive · L2   Open program →
Tezos Foundation Ecosystem Grants 💵 CASH  VIABLE
Tezos Foundation  •  Varies by scope (typically tens of thousands USD-equivalent; milestone-based)  •  Rolling submission; reviewed in the month following each quarter end
Submissions accepted at any time and reviewed after each quarter close. Grant areas include Infrastructure & Tooling and Arts & Culture, making it viable for AI-integrated collaboration or creative-tech tooling that touches Tezos.
VIABLE: Non-dilutive, rolling, for-profit eligible. Next review window follows the Q2 close (~end June 2026) — submit before then. Requires a genuine Tezos integration angle.
web3 · public-goods · tooling · non-dilutive   Open program →
Aleph Zero Ecosystem Funding Program 💵 CASH  VIABLE
Aleph Zero Foundation  •  Fast Track $10K–$30K; larger Growth/Public Goods/Nest tracks from a ~$45M pool  •  Rolling (Fast Track decisions in days/weeks)
Multi-track ecosystem grants (Fast Track, Public Goods, Growth, Nest) funding projects that build capabilities and adoption on Aleph Zero. Fast Track offers quick $10–30K non-dilutive grants on a rolling basis.
VIABLE: No equity, rolling, for-profit teams welcome — but requires building something on Aleph Zero. Apply to Fast Track if you can scope an AI-x-onchain integration; otherwise low fit.
web3 · grants · ecosystem · privacy · for-profit-ok   Open program →
FedEx Small Business Grant Contest 💵 CASH  VIABLE
FedEx  •  Up to $50,000 ($50K grand prize, $30K 2nd, ten $15K 3rd-place)  •  Annual contest; 2026 cycle dates not yet announced (historically opens Feb–March)
Annual contest awarding non-dilutive cash to innovative U.S. small businesses. No equity; application is a relatively light online entry plus voting.
VIABLE: For-profit, no equity, light application — but requires a U.S. entity and an active FedEx shipping account (the main hurdle). Set up FedEx shipping and watch fedex.com/grantcontest for the next window.
startup · small-business · non-dilutive · us-only · cash   Open program →
Skip Instant Grants 💵 CASH  VIABLE
Skip (HelloSkip)  •  Recurring $1,000 cash grants (plus larger periodic $10K+ Skip grants)  •  Rolling — drawings/awards twice weekly, no fixed deadline
A small-business grant marketplace that runs its own recurring micro-grants awarded multiple times per week on a rolling basis. Very low-effort once you complete the dashboard profile.
VIABLE: Smallest cash here, but truly low-effort and rolling for a U.S. entity — sign up, complete the profile once, stay in recurring drawings. Treat as passive supplementary cash, not a primary source.
startup · small-business · micro-grant · rolling · us-only   Open program →
Tether Developer Grants Program 💵 CASH  VIABLE
Tether  •  ~$1,500–$4,000 per task (paid in USD₮ or BTC), no total cap  •  Rolling — claim open tasks anytime
Launched May 11, 2026: bounty-style grants tied to defined tasks across local-first/edge AI, P2P networking, cryptography, libraries, docs, and integrations. Paid on completed work in USD₮ or Bitcoin via tether.dev.
VIABLE: Strong fit — you already run a self-hosted/local AI stack. Pick an open task adjacent to your existing work (edge inference, P2P, tooling) and ship it for a fast payout. Low commitment, real cash.
local-first-ai · edge-ai · web3 · bounty · open-source   Open program →
Rhizome Microgrants 💵 CASH  VIABLE
Rhizome (New Museum affiliate)  •  $500–$1,500 (cash)  •  Next open call expected fall 2026 (annual cycle; 2025–26 round awarded Dec 2025)
Microgrants for experimental work on digital culture, with recent themes including 'Synthetic Agents' (working with AI/other intelligences). Simple open-call application via Submittable; 17 awardees in the last round.
VIABLE: Fits if a team member frames a Castalia/Vox-adjacent experiment as net art on synthetic agents/collaboration. Tiny but trivial to apply. Watch rhizome.org for the next open call (historically opens around September).
creative · digital-art · ai-art · microgrant   Open program →
Alicia's AI Impact Grant 💵 CASH  VIABLE
Alicia (Alicia AI Impact Grant)  •  $2,500 per quarter (cash)  •  June 15, 2026 (this quarter); recurs quarterly
A quarterly $2,500 micro-grant for builders putting AI to work in their business, with an explicitly minimal application ('no credit checks, no long forms'). Funds intended for AI tools, training, or marketing.
VIABLE: Smallest amount on this list but among the lowest-effort applications anywhere — a few-minute form. Apply each quarter; verify the US-residence requirement against your team's situation.
ai · microgrant · low-effort · quarterly   Open program →
ETHGlobal Hackathons 2026 Series 💵 CASH  VIABLE
ETHGlobal  •  $225,000+ in prizes per event (sponsor bounties stack on top)  •  Recurring per event — next major: ETHGlobal New York June 12–14, 2026; then Lisbon Jul 24–26, Tokyo Sep 25–27, Mumbai Q4
ETHGlobal runs a continuous 2026 calendar of in-person and async hackathons (HackMoney, New York, Lisbon, Tokyo, Mumbai) with $225K+ in main prizes plus dozens of stacked sponsor bounties per event. Cash/crypto prizes paid to winning teams with no equity taken.
VIABLE: Direct cash/crypto prizes, no equity, simple application. A 2-10 person AI+web3 team can ship a Castalia/Vox onchain demo and stack multiple sponsor bounties. Action: register for NY June 12 or async rounds.
web3 · hackathon · prize · ethereum · non-dilutive   Open program →
Polkadot Fast-Grants Programme 💵 CASH  VIABLE
Polkadot / Web3 Foundation ecosystem (community-curated)  •  Up to $10,000 USD (paid in DOT), milestone-based  •  Rolling — apply anytime via GitHub PR
Fast, low-friction grants up to $10K in DOT for early-stage builders on Polkadot, paid after milestone delivery. Designed as initial funding that bridges to larger downstream grants or VC. Companies are explicitly eligible.
VIABLE: No equity, for-profit eligible, GitHub-PR application = very low effort. Requires building something on Polkadot — viable if Liminal ships a small onchain/collaboration component. Cash (DOT) on milestone completion.

Aleo Developer Grants Program 💵 CASH  VIABLE
Aleo Network Foundation  •  Up to $100,000 per project  •  Rolling — applications open now
Up to $100K plus ecosystem support for building zero-knowledge applications on Aleo. Supports all stages from experiments to production-ready apps; companies explicitly eligible. Submit project overview, team background, and proposal.
VIABLE: For-profit eligible, no equity, rolling. Needs a privacy/ZK angle built on Aleo — viable if Liminal's self-hosted AI stack ships a privacy-preserving feature on Aleo. Real cash up to $100K.

RAISE the STAKES 2026 (RAISE Summit AI Startup Competition) 💵 CASH  VIABLE
RAISE Summit (Paris)  •  Over €10M in prizes (non-dilutive)  •  Applications close June 1, 2026 (annual; recurs in 2027)
Billed as the world's largest AI startup competition, held July 8–9, 2026 at the Louvre with 840+ VCs and 9,000+ attendees. Top 100 announced June 10; non-dilutive prize pool plus major investor exposure.
VIABLE: Eligibility fits exactly (2+ members, <$10M raised). URGENT: the 2026 application window closes today (June 1) — apply immediately or target the 2027 annual cycle. High effort (pitch competition), uncertain odds, but real non-dilutive cash.

NASE Growth Grants 💵 CASH  VIABLE
National Association for the Self-Employed (NASE)  •  Up to $4,000 cash  •  Rolling — applications accepted year-round, reviewed quarterly (4 winners/quarter)
Straightforward small-business cash grants of up to $4,000 for growth needs, awarded to four members each quarter on a rolling basis. Funds can't be used for debt or rent/mortgage.
VIABLE: Real cash, no equity, very low effort, open to for-profits. Only catch is paid NASE membership (modest annual fee). Apply anytime; quarterly cycles. Good quick-win for a specific spend (e.g. marketing or a hire).

Monad Ecosystem Grants & Momentum Program 💵 CASH  VIABLE
Monad Foundation  •  Grants/incentive matching from a 38.5B MON ecosystem allocation; $40M+ distributed to teams by Q1 2026  •  Rolling
The Monad Foundation issues ecosystem grants and runs Momentum, an incentives-matching program for outstanding apps deploying to Monad mainnet. Funding is in MON/cash to help teams execute their own growth strategies.
VIABLE: Rolling, for-profit-friendly, no equity. Action: ship a Monad-integrated module (e.g. onchain coordination/payments for Castalia or Vox) and apply via the Foundation.

Eigen Foundation (EigenLayer / EigenCloud)  •  Grants from 15% of initial EIGEN supply allocated to community initiatives; awarded in seasons  •  Season-based rolling intake (6-month seasons, retroactively reviewed)
The Eigen Foundation funds developers and contributors building experimental products in the EigenLayer ecosystem, allocated in six-month seasons with retroactive review. A grants portal for Community Initiative Grants is rolling out.
VIABLE: For-profit teams OK, no equity. Action: frame an EigenLayer AVS or restaking-secured coordination service; watch eigenfoundation.org for the grants portal opening.

Microsoft AI Dev Days Hackathon 💵 CASH  VIABLE
Microsoft (Reactor / Azure)  •  $80,000+ total prize pool; $10,000 per category winning team + Build 2026 tickets  •  Next Reactor hackathon iteration expected mid/late 2026 (this edition judged March 2026)
Recurring online Microsoft Reactor hackathon series with cash prizes for AI agents, AI applications, and agentic DevOps. Low-effort relative to grants; the Jan–Mar 2026 round has concluded, and Reactor runs these AI hackathons repeatedly through the year.
VIABLE: Real cash ($10k/category), no equity, fully online, open to any team, modest effort if you already build AI agents — a natural fit for the Castalia/Vox stack. Track the Reactor events page and enter the next AI agents hackathon.

The Freed Fellowship Grant 💵 CASH  VIABLE
Freed Fellowship  •  $500 monthly grant; $2,500 year-end grant for one fellow  •  Rolling — monthly; apply by the last day of each month
A monthly no-strings small-business grant. Every applicant gets a 'Freed score' and feedback; monthly winners get $500 and eligibility for the $2,500 year-end grant.
VIABLE: Cleanest fit: US for-profit small biz, simple form, no equity. Small dollars and a $19 fee, but near-zero effort. Submit each month to stay in the year-end pool.

Secretsos Small Business Grant 💵 CASH  VIABLE
Secretsos Foundation  •  $2,500 per quarter  •  Quarterly — last day of each quarter (next: June 30, 2026)
Quarterly $2,500 cash grant for U.S. small business owners. Narrative-based application (your challenges, community, vision) — no formal business plan required. Windows run Jan/Apr/Jul/Oct, closing the last day of each quarter.
VIABLE: Open to any U.S.-registered for-profit; Liminal Commons qualifies. Very low effort, pure narrative. Apply this quarter before June 30; if missed, reapply Q3 (Jul 1 open).

Global annual startup competition awarding up to $1M in equity-free cash plus mentorship and a finals stage in Riyadh. Open to any sector and stage worldwide.
VIABLE: Equity-free, global, all-sector — strong fit when open. The 2026 window just closed (May 31); put on the radar for the ~early-2027 cycle and prep a pitch now.

START Global (University of St. Gallen)  •  Up to CHF 40,000 equity-free (multiple tracks; one track CHF 10,000 equity-free)  •  Next window: applications expected to close ~early March 2027 ahead of the spring summit
Annual St. Gallen startup summit running equity-free pitching competitions with up to CHF 40,000 in cash prizes plus investor visibility. The 2026 edition (Mar 19–20, apps closed Mar 6) has passed; the next cycle runs spring 2027.
VIABLE: Equity-free cash prize open to for-profit startups in any sector — strong fit. Set a reminder to apply in Jan–Feb 2027 when the next call opens.

Mantle Network (Mantle Foundation)  •  Fast-track $MNT token grants (typically a few thousand to low tens of thousands USD-equivalent)  •  Rolling — applications reviewed weekly
A deliberately low-friction grant track: a sub-10-minute 'ditch the pitch' application reviewed every week. Distinct from Mantle's main Grants Program — built for speed and small experiments rather than large milestone proposals.
VIABLE: No equity, for-profit OK, ~10-min form, weekly review. Easiest possible web3 cash entry — frame Castalia/Vox infra or an onchain integration angle and apply immediately.

Coinbase Developer Platform runs seasonal builder-grant programs rewarding teams shipping with CDP tooling. Distinct from Base Builder Grants — these are CDP-product-specific and application-based.

Non-dilutive grants for teams bringing mobile-first onchain UX to Solana, requiring a clear budget tied to milestones and a mobile launch. Distinct from the general Solana Foundation Grants already tracked.

Newly launched (May 5, 2026) U.S. small-business grant. Five winners receive $5,000 in non-dilutive cash plus mentorship; 25 finalists get resources. Simple online application.

Annual AI startup pitch competition with an US$850k equity-free prize pool. 10 finalists pitch at SuperAI Singapore. 2026 round is done; the 2027 waitlist is open now.

A grant track from Coinbase Developer Platform funding builders shipping AI agents with onchain payment integrations. Small, fast, equity-free — aligns with autonomous-agent/payment features.

ARC Prize 2026 💵 CASH  VIABLE
ARC Prize Foundation  •  $2,000,000 total prize pool (milestone prizes: 1st $25K, 2nd $10K, 3rd $2.5K per milestone; ARC-AGI-2 $1M grand prize; Paper Track $450K)  •  Milestone #1: June 30, 2026; Milestone #2: Sept 30, 2026 (final grand-prize deadline later in 2026)
Public competition to push toward AGI on the ARC-AGI benchmark, with $2M in cash prizes across milestone, grand-prize, and paper tracks. No equity, no affiliation required — pure prize money for a working open-sourced solution.
VIABLE: No equity, no affiliation, for-profit eligible — but technically very hard. Realistic angle: target a milestone prize or the Paper Track rather than the grand prize. Entry itself is low-effort (Kaggle submission); winning is high-effort.

Sequoia Open Source Fellowship 💵 CASH  VIABLE
Sequoia Capital  •  Stipend covering living expenses for 6–12 months (no equity, not an investment)  •  Rolling
A no-strings stipend for developers to work full-time on an open-source project for 6–12 months. Rolling applications, simple process, and Sequoia explicitly disclaims equity or investment rights.
VIABLE: Strong fit if a Liminal Commons component (e.g. a Castalia/Vox open-source library) is positioned as the OSS project. Rolling + simple = low burden. Frame around a dev's dedicated OSS work.

Darcy's Business  •  $1,000 unrestricted cash  •  Final deadline June 30, 2026 (earlier tiers: Mar 31, May 15)
A simple, free micro-grant of $1,000 in unrestricted cash for small businesses to spend on whatever drives growth. Open to virtually anyone; minimal application.

A dedicated AI track of Avalanche's infraBUIDL program funding AI-powered infrastructure on Avalanche (e.g., AI treasury/agent systems). Applications are taken on a rolling basis through the Builder Hub.

A fast, low-friction grants vehicle from Lightcone Infrastructure offering requests of any size with rapid evaluation. Funds high-impact projects, with strong emphasis on AI safety and long-term flourishing.

An annual grant of up to $50K plus in-kind support for projects exploring artistic applications of technology and culture. The 2026 RFP has closed; the program recurs annually with the next call expected in early 2027.

Sei Development Foundation × Gitcoin  •  $250k matching pool (quadratic funding round)  •  Round opens June 4, 2026 — apply via Gitcoin Grants Stack
A $250k quadratic-funding round (part of a larger $10M Creator Fund) run on Gitcoin Grants Stack to bootstrap projects on Sei — matching is driven by breadth of small community donations.
PARTIAL: On-mandate web3/QF monitoring item. Only viable with a genuine Sei-ecosystem angle (e.g., an integration or onchain feature) — don't force it. If you have one, the QF mechanism rewards rallying your community to make many small donations.
web3 · quadratic-funding · public-goods · gitcoin   Open program →
Interledger Foundation Fellowship 2026 💵 CASH  PARTIAL
Interledger Foundation  •  $72,000 stipend + $20,000 project budget  •  June 15, 2026 (11:59 EDT) — applications open via Submittable since April 1, 2026
A 12-month fellowship supporting individuals building or researching solutions that help underserved populations access digital financial systems. Compensation is a $72K stipend plus a $20K project budget — real cash, not credits.
PARTIAL: Awarded to an individual, not a company, and needs an open-payments / financial-inclusion angle. A team member could apply with a Vox/Castalia-adjacent open-payments project. Open right now — closes June 15. Act fast.

Renaissance Philanthropy (with XTX Markets)  •  $100,000 – $1,000,000 (12–24 months of work)  •  First-round deadline was April 10, 2026; reviewed on a rolling basis with additional funding announced — next open call expected 2026
Funds AI-for-mathematics work — automated theorem proving, mathematical reasoning, training datasets/benchmarks, and tooling. Grants $100K–$1M. XTX Markets added $13.5M in May 2026, signaling continued open calls.
PARTIAL: Requires a genuine AI-for-math research angle, which is a stretch for a collaboration-platform team. Viable only if Liminal frames a reasoning/benchmark tooling project. Watch for the next open call; rolling review when open.

Shuttleworth Foundation Fellowship 💵 CASH  PARTIAL
Shuttleworth Foundation  •  Salary-equivalent stipend + $275,000/year project funding per Fellow  •  Continuous intake; cohorts start March 1 and September 1 (Sept intake closes ~May 1). Next applicable window: 2027 intakes
Funds an individual's salary for a year plus $275K/year of project funding to pursue an open-knowledge/open-tech idea for social change. Among the most generous open-tech fellowships; recurring biannual intakes.
PARTIAL: Goes to an individual (full-time commitment) and needs an openness/social-change framing, not a for-profit product pitch. A founder could apply with an open-knowledge angle on Liminal's platforms. Next intake window 2027.

A $200K prize recognizing and funding breakthrough AI solutions that benefit humanity, awarded annually by the McGovern Foundation. Part of the foundation's $75.8M AI-for-public-purpose commitment.

Atom Accelerator DAO (mandated by the Cosmos Hub)  •  $10,000 – $1,000,000 (paid in USDC)  •  Rolling — continuous applications
DAO-run grant program funding Cosmos Hub ecosystem work, paying grants in USDC (real cash, not vesting tokens). Range spans small experiments to large multi-phase builds, plus a venture funding track.

Interchain Foundation (ICF)  •  CHF 50,000 – 300,000 (infra & tooling)  •  Rolling — continuous applications
ICF funds work that strengthens the Cosmos Network and IBC via grants, deliverable-based service agreements, or investments. Application requires a description, budget, and timeline at apply.interchain.io.

The Hopper Prize  •  2 grants of $4,500 + 4 grants of $1,000 (unrestricted)  •  Recurring — twice yearly; next is the Fall 2026 cycle (Spring closed May 12, 2026)
Unrestricted cash grants for visual/new-media artists via a short (~20 min) open-call application. Awards are fully unrestricted; winners also get exposure to a 150k+ audience.

Minimal, artist-friendly unrestricted micro-grant awarded three times a year with no written proposal — among the lowest-effort cash applications available.

Two parallel tracks: the Developer Grants Program (committee-reviewed, rolling, for tooling/research/education) and Flow GrantDAO (community MACI-voted rounds, up to 50k FLOW per project). Both pay in FLOW tokens.

Rolling research awards funding empirical work on AI's economic effects, with findings to be shared within 6 months. Includes Claude API credits and access to Anthropic Economic Index data.

Global Warming Mitigation Project  •  $50,000 cash to each of 10 winners  •  Annual — 2026 cycle closed Jan 15, 2026; next (2027) cycle expected to open late 2026
Annual prize awarding $50,000 each to 10 initiatives that help bend the Keeling Curve downward. The 'Social and Cultural Pathways' category funds solutions that influence human behavior and climate action.

Visa's global startup competition challenges growth-stage startups to build payment and commerce innovations, with cash prizes, a virtual immersion program, and potential Visa pilot collaboration. 2026 is the largest edition with multiple regional competitions feeding a global final.

XTC is a large global startup competition connecting founders to investors, corporates and mentors, culminating in global finals. It is equity-free and backed by Microsoft, Intel, Cisco, ARM and others; 'enabling technologies' (incl. AI platforms) is an eligible category.

q/acc (Quadratic Accelerator) 💵 CASH  PARTIAL
Giveth / Quadratic Accelerator (Polygon, GLM ecosystem)  •  $50,000 grant + $100,000 community matching pool per season  •  Rolling seasonal cohorts; applications open between seasons
Web3 token-launch accelerator combining quadratic funding with augmented bonding curves. Accepted projects get a $50K grant to tokenize plus a season-end q/acc round with a $100K matching pool to bootstrap token liquidity and community.
PARTIAL: Real cash for for-profit web3 teams, but requires committing to a token launch via ABC — only viable if we frame a tokenized public-goods component. Watch qacc.giveth.io for the next season's open call.
web3 · quadratic funding · public goods · tokenization · accelerator   Open program →
Arbitrum Trailblazer AI Grant Program 💵 CASH  PARTIAL
Arbitrum Foundation  •  Up to $10,000 per project (from a $1M pool); Trailblazer 2.0 adds agentic-DeFi track  •  Rolling — applications reviewed on an ongoing basis
A $1M initiative giving fast grants (up to $10K each) to builders of specialized AI agents and onchain AI products on Arbitrum. Trailblazer 2.0 specifically targets agentic-DeFi strategies built with the Vibekit agent framework.
PARTIAL: Direct fit for an AI team, but requires shipping a live AI agent with an Arbitrum onchain integration first. Low-effort form once a qualifying agent exists — good for a focused web3 spin-off of our agent stack.

Kalshi (co-sponsored by Solana Foundation & Base)  •  $2M+ in grants plus builder-code volume revenue, engineering & marketing support  •  Rolling — ongoing since September launch
Kalshi's ecosystem hub offers $2M+ in grants to teams building on its tokenized prediction markets — trading bots, analytics dashboards, data viz, mobile apps, extensions — plus a builder code that earns ongoing revenue on facilitated volume.
PARTIAL: Cash + revenue share for for-profit builders, but scoped to Kalshi prediction-market integrations — viable only if we build a relevant onchain tool. Simple rolling application.

EDA AI Upskill Accelerator Pilot Program 💵 CASH  PARTIAL
U.S. Department of Commerce — Economic Development Administration  •  $1M–$8M per award (5–8 awards, ~$25M total); up to 60% federal share  •  September 30, 2026
New $25M NOFO funding AI workforce-training projects tied to regional economies. Lead applicant must convene an employer-led sectoral partnership delivering AI-skills training; projects run 24–36 months.
PARTIAL: For-profits typically can't be the lead EDA applicant — would require partnering with an eligible econ-dev/nonprofit/higher-ed lead and providing the AI training tech/platform. High effort but large cash awards. US-only.

LEAP / One Giant Leap (Saudi Arabia)  •  Equity-free cash: $70K winner / $20K runner-up / $10K third ($100K pool)  •  Next cycle expected for LEAP 2027 (last window closed May 20, 2026)
Annual equity-free pitch competition at LEAP, one of the largest global tech events. Top startups from the LEAP Startup Programme compete for a $100K equity-free cash prize pool on the main stage.
PARTIAL: Equity-free cash with no IP strings, but requires acceptance into the LEAP program and travel/exhibition in Saudi Arabia — meaningful logistics. Watch for the LEAP 2027 startup program call (typically opens late 2026).

Long-running annual competition (since 2007, $3.4M+ awarded) offering $100K+ in non-dilutive cash prizes plus mentoring to early-stage climate and energy ventures from anywhere in the world.
PARTIAL: Non-dilutive cash open to global for-profit teams, but you need a genuine climate/energy angle (e.g., AI-for-grid/efficiency framing). 2026 form already closed; the 2026–27 application goes live in the fall.

RFP funding work that reduces the risk of AI driving extreme concentration of power — covering technical, governance, and tooling angles. Rolling review with a firm July 2, 2026 deadline.

Independent $1M fund managed by Vite's core team to pay maintainers and fund popular plugins/tooling across the Vite stack. Cash to support independent ecosystem work, not equity.

The Hashgraph Association Grant Program ◐ CASH + CREDITS  PARTIAL
The Hashgraph Association (Hedera)  •  Up to $250,000 co-investment for startups (up to $500k enterprise)  •  Rolling — applications accepted year-round
Non-profit ecosystem fund providing milestone-based co-investment funding plus venture-building support for teams building production solutions on the Hedera network. 6–8 week review from application to board decision.
PARTIAL: Requires building on Hedera and co-investment/milestone structure. Viable if Castalia/Vox add a Hedera-anchored component (e.g., onchain provenance or payments). Action: scope a minimal Hedera integration and apply via the form.

Build On Cronos — Ignition Program ◐ CASH + CREDITS  PARTIAL
Cronos Labs / Cronos.org ($100M ecosystem fund)  •  Up to $200,000 support + ~$2,000/mo microgrants (1 yr) + 50% gas-fee revenue share  •  Rolling — applications open via Tally form
Cronos refocused its $100M fund into Build On Cronos; the Ignition track backs early native founders with up to $200K, recurring monthly microgrants, and gas-fee revenue share. AI Agents is one of four explicit focus domains.
PARTIAL: Strong fit on the AI-agents focus area, but requires building on Cronos. Action: prototype a Cronos-deployed AI-agent feature and apply through the Tally form; microgrants give steady non-dilutive runway.

GitHub Accelerator (2026 Cohort) ◐ CASH + CREDITS  PARTIAL
GitHub  •  $20,000 sponsorship + Azure AI/GPU credits + go-to-market support  •  2026 cohort applications open now
10-week cohort for open-source maintainers: $20K upfront sponsorship plus workshops, mentorship, and free Azure AI infrastructure (incl. high-end GPU clusters). 2026 cohort emphasizes advancing AI in the open.
PARTIAL: $20K cash, no equity, team of up to 3 — strong fit IF you apply with an open-source project. Liminal would need to open-source a meaningful component (e.g. a Castalia/Vox library or self-hosted AI-stack tool) to qualify. Low-medium effort.
open-source · AI · accelerator · no-equity · cash   Open program →
Base Batches 2026 ◐ CASH + CREDITS  PARTIAL
Base (Coinbase) Ecosystem Fund  •  $10k non-dilutive grant to top 15 Startup Track teams + 8-week program; $50k investment to a minimum of 3 teams  •  Recurring cohort program (2026 edition)
Base Batches combines structured development, mentorship and funding. Top 15 Startup Track teams get a $10k non-dilutive grant and an 8-week virtual program; at least 3 teams receive a $50k investment from the Base Ecosystem Fund.
PARTIAL: The $10k grant is non-dilutive; the $50k track is an investment (dilutive). Action: apply Startup Track with a Base-deployed product to win the grant without taking the investment.

AWS Agentic AI Call for Proposals (Amazon Research Awards) ◐ CASH + CREDITS  PARTIAL
Amazon / AWS  •  Up to ~$70k unrestricted funds + up to ~$50k AWS promotional credits (per award, on average)  •  Next call expected Fall 2026 (Spring 2026 window closed May 13, 2026)
Recurring Amazon Research Awards track funding agentic-AI work with unrestricted cash plus AWS credits. The Spring 2026 cycle (Mar 25–May 13) has closed; ARA runs multiple cycles per year, so a next call is expected.
PARTIAL: Mix of unrestricted cash and AWS credits, but ARA usually requires an academic PI — viable only via a university collaborator. Watch for the next cycle and partner with an academic co-PI on agentic-AI for collaboration.

Scroll Community Grants Program ◐ CASH + CREDITS  PARTIAL
Scroll Foundation  •  Variable (program pilot budget ~SCR 312,500); milestone/proposal-based in SCR  •  Ongoing — Community Council reviews proposals within 7 days
Web3 ecosystem grants for projects that build community, drive adoption, and create impact on Scroll. Includes a 'creative proposals' track (education, scholarships, novel projects) beyond standard event funding, with fast 7-day council review.
PARTIAL: Viable only if you build/deploy something on Scroll or run a developer/education initiative there — requires an ecosystem angle. Paid in SCR token. Low effort, fast review. Confirm current intake cadence on portal.

Alchemy-Arbitrum Grant Program ◐ CASH + CREDITS  PARTIAL
Alchemy (with Arbitrum Foundation)  •  $10M pool for Orbit chain development; up to $500K in infrastructure credits per team  •  Rolling (Typeform application)
Joint program offering grants plus up to $500K in Alchemy infrastructure credits per team to teams launching Orbit chains or scaling on Arbitrum. Mix of cash-equivalent support and substantial dev-infra credits.
PARTIAL: Best fit if Liminal stands up an app-specific Orbit chain or heavy onchain infra. Mostly credits, so lower priority than pure-cash items.

Linea Consortium (Consensys, ENS Labs, Eigen Labs, Status)  •  Ecosystem Fund = 75% of LINEA supply; ~25% deployed for activation in first 12–18 months (grants + strategic funding)  •  Grants program opening in 2026 (rolling once live); LEIA funding intake open now
The Linea Consortium is standing up the largest ecosystem fund in the space, funding protocol R&D, shared infrastructure, open-source tools, and mission-aligned builders. The grants track is non-dilutive; the LEIA alliance side is investment.
PARTIAL: Strong fit if any Liminal product (collaboration infra, AI agents) ships onchain tooling on Linea. Grants track is no-equity; watch for the formal grants program launch and apply early. LEIA path is equity.

$200K builder-grant program from Startale Cloud Services and thirdweb to support teams shipping on Soneium, Sony's Optimism-based L2. Combines development support with grant funding for live/launching apps.

Databricks / Databricks Ventures  •  $1M+ total: up to $600K Databricks Ventures investment + up to $500K product credits across finalists  •  Next cohort expected ~Spring 2027 (2026 window closed May 22, 2026)
Global annual challenge for early-stage B2B startups building on Databricks, with $1M+ in prizes — Databricks Ventures investment plus large product credits — and judging on platform usage, market potential, and team.
PARTIAL: Mostly equity investment + credits rather than non-dilutive cash, and requires building core product on Databricks (conflicts with our self-hosted stack). Lower priority; recurs annually with the next window expected in spring 2027.

Google AI Futures Fund ◐ CASH + CREDITS  PARTIAL
Google DeepMind / Google Labs  •  Direct equity investment (varies) + Google Cloud credits + early access to Gemini/Imagen/Veo and DeepMind researcher support  •  Rolling — no fixed window, opportunities considered continuously
A rolling program where Google DeepMind/Labs provides early model access, hands-on technical support, Cloud credits, and the option of direct equity investment. Good for an AI-integrated product team wanting frontier-model access; the cash side is dilutive.
PARTIAL: Rolling and open to for-profits — low effort to express interest. But the cash component is an equity investment (dilutive); the non-dilutive value is credits + model/eng support. Apply if you want DeepMind access; don't count on it as non-dilutive cash.
AI · model-access · cloud-credits · rolling · investment   Open program →
Big if True Science Accelerator (BiTS) ◐ CASH + CREDITS  PARTIAL
Renaissance Philanthropy (with SPRIND and regional partners)  •  No direct grant — 15-week part-time accelerator to design and pitch fundable ARPA-style R&D programs (pathway to large program funding)  •  2026 cohort applications open now (Americas/EU/UK/Japan cohorts via Airtable); no fixed public deadline stated
A part-time accelerator run by ex-DARPA program managers that helps technologists turn bold R&D concepts into fundable, ARPA-style programs (2026 cohort tied to SPRIND's Challenge model). Not direct cash, but a structured route into large non-dilutive R&D funding.
PARTIAL: No money changes hands directly — it builds and de-risks a fundable program proposal and connects you to funders like SPRIND. Worth it only if you have a genuinely frontier R&D agenda. Effort medium; deadline mechanics unclear from the page (rolling Airtable form).

The Pitch by Deel 📈 EQUITY  PARTIAL
Deel  •  $50,000 SAFE (up to 100 regional winners); $1,000,000 SAFE (up to 10 global champions)  •  Next cycle expected early 2027 (2026 global finals were May 18–19, 2026)
Annual global pitch competition with regional finals and a global final awarding SAFE investments up to $1M. Very low-effort application; 2026 cycle has concluded, with the next edition expected to open in early 2027.
PARTIAL: SAFE = convertible (dilutive on conversion), so not truly non-dilutive, but the application is trivially low-effort and global with no geo limits. Worth a 3-minute submission when the next cycle opens; treat as optional dilutive upside, not grant cash.

Avalanche Foundation (Blizzard)  •  $200M+ ecosystem fund (capital deployed into projects)  •  Rolling
Large always-open fund supporting Avalanche-ecosystem projects with capital and institutional support. Listed via the Avalanche Builder Hub grants portal alongside Retro9000 and research calls.
LIMITED: This is investment capital (equity/token), not non-dilutive — fails the no-equity priority. Included only as a rolling Avalanche option; for non-dilutive Avalanche cash, target Retro9000 (already in your DB) instead.

Entrepreneur First — Fellowship / Venture Program 📈 EQUITY  LIMITED
Entrepreneur First (EF)  •  Monthly talent-investment stipend during the build phase (e.g., SGD 5,000/mo in Singapore) + pre-seed investment  •  June 22, 2026 (final selection round for upcoming cohort)
Talent investor that pays a living stipend during cofounder-matching and idea validation, then invests pre-seed. Stipend is real cash runway during the program, but EF takes equity on investment.
LIMITED: EF invests in individuals forming new companies and takes equity — a poor fit for an existing 2–10 person for-profit, and the stipend requires full-time commitment to the cohort. Listed for completeness; dilutive and not aligned with shipping Castalia/Vox.

Non-dilutive cash for investigative reporting expenses on any topic. Seed grants fund preliminary reporting; Regular grants fund full investigations. 2026 cycle dates were Jan 30, Apr 27, and Sept 14.

Just Tech Fellowship 💵 CASH  VIABLE
Social Science Research Council (SSRC)  •  Up to $60,000 unrestricted (plus up to $5,000 for collaborative work)  •  June 28, 2026
One-year, fully-funded fellowship giving individuals an unrestricted ~$60k award to pursue research, creative practice, or community-engaged work on technology and society. Confirmed open for the 2027 cohort with a single application window closing June 28, 2026.
VIABLE: Open to individuals with no institution required; strong fit for AI/tech researchers and builders. Competitive but a single clean application — apply before June 28, 2026.

AI and Society Fellowship 💵 CASH  VIABLE
Center for AI Safety (CAIS)  •  $25,000 stipend (fully-funded 3-month program)  •  Next cohort expected early 2027 (last deadline March 24, 2026)
A fully-funded, autonomy-driven fellowship where individual fellows pursue self-directed AI-and-society research embedded in the SF AI research community, with a $25k stipend. The 2026 window has closed; the program runs annually and a next cohort is expected.
VIABLE: Open to individuals with no institution required; strong fit for AI researchers. 2026 deadline (March 24) passed — watch safe.ai/fellowship for the next annual call (~early 2027).

The Mill at Vicksburg (Prairie Ronde, Michigan)  •  $2,000 completion stipend + $500 travel grant + private housing + gallery show  •  Next cycle expected early 2027 (2026 deadline March 1, 2026)
Funded 5–6 week artist residency providing a $2,000 stipend, $500 travel grant, private housing, and a gallery show. The 2026 cycle closed March 1; the program recurs annually with multiple seasonal sessions.
VIABLE: Open to individual artists with a simple application and no institution required. 2026 closed; watch for the next annual call (~early 2027).

A full-year paid residency ($70K, monthly installments) plus a separate $25K fellowship for emerging writers, hosted at the Boston Public Library. No institutional affiliation required — apply directly via Submittable with a 5-page proposal and 15-page sample.

Small, low-friction microgrants (via a Google Form) for interactive-fiction and creative-tech projects — well-suited to indie devs and creators experimenting with AI-driven narrative tools.

World Photography Organisation / Sony  •  Up to $25,000 cash (Photographer of the Year) + $5,000 category prizes; free to enter  •  January 12, 2027 (Series); rolling single-image categories close earlier
The 20th-anniversary edition is open for entries. Top prize is $25,000 cash; the contest is entirely free to enter, making it a genuinely low-cost shot at real money for photographers, including AI-assisted/computational image work.

Funded residency to create a limited-edition artist book in WSW's studios (papermaking, letterpress, screenprint, etc.), with full production support, housing, and a weekly stipend. Confirmed open with a July 15, 2026 deadline on the program page.

One of the longest-running fully-funded visual-arts residencies in the US: artists get a year of housing, studio, and a monthly stipend in Roswell, NM. The 2026 window closed March 15; the next annual cycle opens Jan 1, 2027 with a March 15 deadline.

Getty Images' creative grant funds individual photographers to pursue personal projects of creative and commercial significance, with the option to license resulting work. Open to individuals globally; check the grants portal for the exact current-cycle deadline.

The world's largest panoramic/wide-format photography competition, with a 2026 edition adding a new Aerial/drone category. Cash and product prizes across Open and Amateur divisions. Confirmed open with final deadline July 13, 2026.

Twice-yearly $500 microgrants to women worldwide to launch or grow an impactful idea, implemented within six months. Simple online application; spring cycle just closed, fall cycle opens September.

Residency fellowship (2–6 fellows/year) hosting researchers and writers in the House of Grímur in Iceland's Westfjords, covering travel, accommodation, and a daily stipend.

A UK-based agency runs monthly micro-grants giving an individual podcaster up to £1,000 in cash plus support. Applications open and close each month via the Content is Queen Marketplace, so it is genuinely low-effort and recurring.

Alicia Patterson Foundation (now within Fund for Investigative Journalism)  •  $20,000 (6-month) or $40,000 (12-month)  •  October 1, 2026 (for 2027 fellowships)
A substantial individual reporting fellowship paying a stipend to pursue an in-depth project, requiring four published articles over the fellowship term. Now administered by FIJ after a 2025 merger.

Open Source Endowment (OSE)  •  ~$5,000 microgrants  •  Rolling — ongoing community nominations
The first dedicated endowment fund for open-source software (501(c)(3), launched early 2026) that distributes investment income as ~$5k microgrants to high-value, at-risk independent projects identified via a Value-Risk framework. Projects are nominated by the community.

A fully-funded remote fellowship paying a stipend to conduct AI governance research using CIP's unique global public-opinion datasets, with co-authorship and mentorship. Open to individuals worldwide, no institutional affiliation required.

Rolling-review microgrants for individual artists pursuing milestone professional-development opportunities. Covers travel, materials, equipment, fees, and childcare. The 2025–26 cycle (two rounds) is closing; a new cycle is expected for the next program year.

Flexible core funding distributed via a participatory decision-making process to grassroots youth-led climate movements. Annual open call; awards support local and national-level organizing.

Thiel Foundation  •  $250,000 over 2 years (no equity taken)  •  Rolling — applications accepted year-round

Grants for Artist Projects (GAP) 💵 CASH  PARTIAL
Artist Trust  •  $2,500 unrestricted (raised from $1,500 in 2026); 65 awards  •  June 22, 2026
Unrestricted $2,500 cash grants for individual artists in any discipline across Washington State, usable for fees, materials, travel, professional development, or to start/continue a project. Currently open with payment in October 2026.
PARTIAL: Open to solo artists with a simple individual application, but restricted to Washington State residents. If WA-based, apply before June 22, 2026.
creative · artist · unrestricted · state-grant · open-now   Open program →
Black Artists Grant (BAG) 💵 CASH  PARTIAL
Creative Debuts  •  £500 per month (one recipient selected monthly)  •  Rolling — no deadline; recipients chosen monthly
An unconditional £500 monthly microgrant for UK-based Black artists, with no reporting requirements and no restriction on use (new work, equipment, travel, or living costs). One application is kept on file and considered every month.
PARTIAL: Extremely low-effort one-time application that stays in the monthly pool, but eligibility is limited to UK-based Black artists.
creative · microgrant · unconditional · rolling · UK   Open program →
Creativity Grants for Projects 💵 CASH  PARTIAL
Maryland State Arts Council (MSAC)  •  $1,000 – $4,000  •  Rolling monthly review; current cycle ends Mar 31, 2026 — next cycle opens July 1, 2026
State arts-council grants of $1,000–$4,000 to independent artists for project planning/development or implementation, accepted on a rolling basis with monthly reviews each program year.
PARTIAL: Open to individual artists with a straightforward rolling application, but restricted to Maryland-connected applicants. New program-year cycle opens July 1, 2026.

Centennial Center Research Grants 💵 CASH  PARTIAL
American Political Science Association (APSA)  •  Up to $2,500 (research); up to $10,000 (conferences/workshops)  •  Recurring — Summer 2026 closed June 1; Spring 2027 cycle opens Jan 1, 2027 (deadline ~Mar 15, 2027)
Over $100,000/year in small research grants to APSA members in two annual cycles. The summer cycle explicitly welcomes independent and unaffiliated scholars; awards support uncompleted political-science research.
PARTIAL: No institutional affiliation required for the summer cycle, but APSA membership is mandatory and projects must be political-science research. Plan for the Spring 2027 cycle (opens January 2027).

The British Academy (with the Leverhulme Trust)  •  Up to £10,000 (tenable up to 24 months)  •  Recurring annual round — latest deadline was May 27, 2026; next round expected to open autumn 2026
Up to £10,000 for primary research in the humanities and social sciences — fieldwork, archives, travel, consumables — open to independent scholars as principal investigators. Runs as a once-a-year competitive round.
PARTIAL: Independent scholars can apply as PI, but the round is competitive and annual with UK-eligibility rules. Watch for the next call opening in autumn 2026.

Accelerate Fellowship 💵 CASH  PARTIAL
Inevitable Foundation  •  $30,000 unrestricted grant + 4-month mentored writing sprint  •  June 17, 2026
Five disabled screenwriters each receive a $30,000 unrestricted cash grant plus a four-month mentored writing sprint to advance a project. Applications confirmed OPEN now (May 28–June 17, 2026); semi-finalist interviews July–August, fellows announced August.
PARTIAL: Individual-eligible and simple to start (eligibility questionnaire + application), but you must be a disabled mid-career film/TV writer meeting one industry criterion. Apply before June 17 if you qualify.

Entry-tier grants of up to $20,000 for early-stage individual explorers to lead a one-year project incorporating science, storytelling, and/or education. Applications run in recurring windows through an online portal.

Oak Spring Garden Foundation  •  $10,000 individual grant + 2–5 week residency  •  Annual; 2026 cycle closed May 31, 2026 — portal reopens ~Feb 2027
Awards one early-career artist a $10,000 unrestricted grant plus a 2–5 week residency at Oak Spring. Lightweight application (CV, three short statements, work samples). 2026 portal closed May 31; reopens annually around February.

The Amphion Foundation  •  Grants supporting recording projects of contemporary concert/serious music  •  Fall cycle ~mid-September 2026 (annual)
Amphion's main grant program funds organizations, but its Elliott Carter Special Grant Program is the one avenue open to individuals — supporting recording projects of contemporary serious/concert music. Fall cycle deadline is annually around mid-September.
PARTIAL: One of the few Amphion paths open to individuals; niche (serious/contemporary music recordings). Action: confirm the exact fall 2026 deadline on the program page before assembling a recording proposal.

Small, fast micro-grants seeding early climate ideas, with a low-burden application and global eligibility. Useful for an individual builder testing a climate concept; cash is modest but the application is light.

Global award + accelerator for under-25 climate leaders. Free to enter; applicants submit a short video and evidence of their project's impact. Open right now with a firm late-June deadline.

Perplexity AI  •  Share of a $42.5M payout pool (80/20 revenue split to creators/publishers)  •  Rolling onboarding
Launched January 2026, this program pays creators/publishers 80% of revenue when their content is cited, indexed, or used by Perplexity's assistant, drawn from a $42.5M pool. Rolling onboarding for qualifying content producers.

AARP Community Challenge 💵 CASH  VIABLE

Funds quick-action 'livable communities' projects — public places, transportation, housing, digital connections, disaster resilience. Over $8M awarded annually; low-burden application.

W.K. Kellogg Foundation  •  Varies (project / program support)  •  Rolling (year-round, by invitation after concept)
Accepts grant applications year-round to support children, families, and racial-equity-focused community work. A submitted concept may be invited to a full proposal — a major national funder with a rolling front door.
PARTIAL: Highly competitive and mission-specific (children/family/equity); rolling concept submission lowers the barrier to a first touch, but expect a substantial proposal if invited. Confirm current priority areas before submitting.
children · families · equity · community · rolling   Open program →
Bank of America Neighborhood Builders 💵 CASH  PARTIAL
Bank of America Charitable Foundation  •  $100,000–$400,000 (unrestricted, paid over two years) plus leadership training  •  July 1, 2026
Flagship corporate program awarding large two-year unrestricted grants plus executive leadership training to two nonprofits in each participating market. The 2026 application window is open now (June 1 – July 1, 2026).
PARTIAL: Open to small/mid nonprofits but requires 501(c)(3) and is competitive/market-based. Confirm your city participates; apply June–July via the BofA grant portal.
nonprofit · unrestricted · capacity building · community · leadership   Open program →
The Lawrence Foundation Grant 💵 CASH  PARTIAL
The Lawrence Foundation  •  $5,000–$10,000  •  October 31, 2026 (December cycle)
Twice-yearly foundation grants (June and December cycles) supporting environmental and human-services programs via the Common Grant Application. The April 30 (June) cycle has closed; the next deadline is October 31, 2026.
PARTIAL: Needs 501(c)(3) or fiscal sponsor. Low–medium effort via the standard Common Grant Application. Prepare now for the October 31, 2026 deadline.
environment · human services · small grant · 501c3 · fiscal sponsor   Open program →
WomensNet Amber Grant 💵 CASH  PARTIAL
WomensNet  •  $10,000 awarded monthly (multiple winners), plus larger year-end grants for monthly recipients  •  Rolling — applications close the last day of each month
WomensNet awards multiple $10,000 grants every month to women-owned businesses, with monthly winners eligible for larger year-end awards. One short online application per month; very low burden.
PARTIAL: Best fit for women-led ventures or revenue-generating women-led nonprofits (not pure grantmakers). Apply any month; reapplication carries into future months.
women-owned · small business · microgrant · rolling · low-effort   Open program →
Nathan Cummings Foundation — Advancement Grants 💵 CASH  PARTIAL
Nathan Cummings Foundation  •  Up to $250,000/year for up to two years  •  Rolling — Letters of Inquiry accepted year-round
Project-based or scaling grants of up to $250k/year via a year-round Letter of Inquiry portal. Light-touch entry (LOI first) before any full proposal is invited.
PARTIAL: Strong fit for mission-driven nonprofits if work maps to REEJ themes. Start with the low-effort LOI any time; full proposal only on invitation.
justice · racial equity · climate · nonprofit · rolling   Open program →
Pulitzer Center — U.S. Local News Reporting Grants 💵 CASH  PARTIAL
Pulitzer Center  •  Project-based reporting grant (amount scaled to project; travel, records, production costs)  •  Rolling — applications reviewed as received
Rolling cash support for ambitious U.S. local-news reporting projects, with decisions typically within a month. Distinct from the Center's Global Reporting Grants.
PARTIAL: Open to journalists and newsroom collaborations (no 501(c)(3) required), but proposals are competitive and story-specific. Pitch a concrete project any time.
journalism · local news · reporting · rolling · freelance   Open program →
ProPublica Local Reporting Network 💵 CASH  PARTIAL
ProPublica  •  Reporter salary reimbursed up to $80,000 plus a benefits stipend (one year)  •  Rolling — apply no later than July 27, 2026
ProPublica funds a full-time reporter at a local newsroom for a year of investigative accountability work, reimbursing salary up to $80k plus benefits and supplying senior editing and data resources.
PARTIAL: For established local newsrooms (not individuals); high-effort proposal but substantial cash. Apply before the July 27, 2026 rolling cutoff.
journalism · investigative · newsroom · accountability · salary support   Open program →
Civic Engagement Microgrant Program 💵 CASH  PARTIAL
Research!America  •  Up to $4,000 per project  •  2025–2026 cycle closed; 2026–2027 application details published Fall 2026
Microgrants supporting early-career-scientist-led groups building local civic-science initiatives that engage communities in two-way dialogue about science and research. Highly competitive (17 of 132 funded in 2026).
PARTIAL: Fits org-affiliated student/early-career teams doing civic science, not general nonprofits. Action: check the site in Fall 2026 when 2026–2027 guidelines publish; low-effort small application.

Transformative Climate Communities (TCC) Round 6 💵 CASH  PARTIAL
California Strategic Growth Council / Department of Conservation  •  Planning, Project Development & Implementation grants (Implementation awards historically $1M–$35M)  •  Pre-Proposal due June 30, 2026
State climate-equity program funding community-led greenhouse-gas-reduction and resilience projects. Round 6 (FY25-26) Pre-Proposal window is open now with a June 30, 2026 deadline across Planning, Project Development, and Implementation tracks.
PARTIAL: Open to 501(c)(3)s and tribally-owned nonprofits, but California-only and a competitive, document-heavy RFP. Best as lead applicant or coalition partner on a place-based project. Action: confirm geography eligibility and start the Pre-Proposal before June 30.
climate · environmental justice · community · California · government   Open program →
Exploring Equitable Futures (Pioneering Ideas CFP) 💵 CASH  PARTIAL
Robert Wood Johnson Foundation (RWJF)  •  Varies (exploratory grants; brief proposal first)  •  Rolling — open year-round
RWJF's standing open call for early, unconventional ideas. Applicants submit a short brief proposal (project description + preliminary budget) on a rolling basis and hear back within 1–6 months.
PARTIAL: Low-effort brief-proposal entry point, open continuously — good for a novel AI-for-health-equity idea. But historically funds <1% of submissions, so frame a genuinely future-oriented angle. Action: register at my.rwjf.org and submit a brief proposal.
health equity · social innovation · civic · research · rolling   Open program →
Public Health Leadership Co-Creation Initiative 💵 CASH  PARTIAL
The Kresge Foundation  •  $100,000 per team  •  Applications open June 6, 2026
New Kresge initiative bringing emerging public-health professionals together with community leaders; each selected team receives a $100,000 grant to develop sustainable, community-driven health solutions. Application portal opens June 6, 2026.
PARTIAL: Open to community-based teams, manageable $100k ask, but requires the rising-leader + community-leader team structure. Action: line up a community-leader partner now and apply when the portal opens June 6.

MacDowell Fellowship 💵 CASH  PARTIAL
MacDowell  •  Residency (no fees) + need-based stipends and travel-reimbursement grants  •  Spring/Summer 2027 cycle: applications open Aug 15, 2026; deadline Sept 10, 2026
Premier artist residency offering uninterrupted time, studio, and meals at no cost, plus need-based living stipends and travel grants. The next open cycle (Spring/Summer 2027 residencies) accepts applications Aug 15–Sept 10, 2026.
PARTIAL: For individual artists, not orgs — relevant if a team member does creative/interdisciplinary work. Need-based stipend means real cash support beyond the residency. Action: prepare a work sample for the Aug 15 opening.

Knight Cities Challenge 💵 CASH  PARTIAL
John S. and James L. Knight Foundation  •  Up to $200,000 per project  •  Annual; 2026 round closed — next cycle expected early 2027
A multi-million-dollar open call (up to $200k per project) for bold ideas that spark local change in Knight's 26 resident communities. The 2026 round (launched April 1, 2026) is closed; the program recurs annually.
PARTIAL: Open to both individuals and orgs and only a short pitch to enter, but restricted to Knight's 26 communities and the 2026 window has closed. Action: confirm your city is on the Knight list and watch for the early-2027 reopening.

2026 Veterans and Nature Grant 💵 CASH  PARTIAL
National Environmental Education Foundation (NEEF)  •  Up to $10,000 per grantee  •  June 10, 2026 (11:59 PM PST)
Funds projects on public lands that address physical and mental health in veteran communities through outdoor activities, wellness, environmental education, adaptive programming, and conservation volunteering. Cash grant, simple portal application.
PARTIAL: Open now and closing soon — needs a nonprofit (or land-manager/tribe) tied to public-lands access. If your community work touches veterans + outdoors, apply before June 10.

Clif Family Foundation — Open Call Grants 💵 CASH  PARTIAL
Clif Family Foundation  •  $5,000–$50,000 (one-year general operating)  •  August 1, 2026 (11:59 PM PST)
Twice-yearly (Mar 1 / Aug 1) open call giving unrestricted general operating support to small mission-driven nonprofits. In 2025 distributed $27.6M across 389 grants. New Temelio application portal with an eligibility quiz.
PARTIAL: Strong fit for a small mission org if your work touches food/climate/justice; needs 501(c)(3). Unrestricted operating money + simple LOI-style form. Next window Aug 1, 2026.

Access Now Grants 💵 CASH  PARTIAL
Access Now  •  Flexible grants (grantee-driven, typically small)  •  Rolling / by invitation and outreach
Flexible, grantee-driven funding for research, advocacy, anti-shutdown campaigns, digital-security accompaniment, and protecting civic space during elections/conflict. Aimed at under-funded at-risk communities.
PARTIAL: Best for orgs in LMICs working on digital rights; flexible/unrestricted cash. Reach out via the grants page — funding is relationship- and outreach-driven rather than a fixed RFP.

CivicHive CivicTech Fellowship 2026 💵 CASH  PARTIAL
CivicHive (BudgIT)  •  Monthly stipend + paid conference trip + technical/legal support  •  Applications open (2026 cohort; demo day July 23, 2026)
A 20-week incubation bootcamp for early-stage civic innovators using tech to improve governance and civic participation. Includes a monthly stipend, mentorship, and technical/legal support; 15 solutions incubated per cohort.
PARTIAL: Real stipend cash for civic-tech teams, but geographically limited to West Africa. Apply if you have a team/resident there; otherwise monitor for future open cohorts.
civic-tech · fellowship · stipend · west-africa · governance   Open program →
Cummings $30 Million Grant Program 💵 CASH  PARTIAL
Cummings Foundation  •  $10,000–$100,000 per year (3-year or 10-year grants)  •  September 17, 2026 (5:00 PM)
Awards $30M to 150 local nonprofits as multi-year (3- or 10-year) general support grants. Process begins with a simple online letter of inquiry; community volunteers run the review.
PARTIAL: Excellent multi-year cash with a low-effort LOI — but strictly for Greater Boston (Essex/Middlesex/Suffolk + 6 Norfolk towns) nonprofits. Apply by Sept 17 if you serve that region.

Digital Freedom Fund — Grant Program 💵 CASH  PARTIAL
Digital Freedom Fund (DFF)  •  €3,000 to €100,000+ (avg €25,000–€45,000)  •  Next biannual call expected H2 2026 (last call closed Feb 17, 2026)
Biannual grants for strategic litigation, pre-litigation research, and initiatives at the intersection of digital and human rights in Europe. The Dec 2025–Feb 2026 call has closed; applications are accepted twice per year.
PARTIAL: Europe-focused; suits orgs doing digital-rights legal/advocacy work. Last window just closed — watch for the next biannual call (expected later in 2026) and prep an LOI now.

Small Grants Program (Greater New Haven) 💵 CASH  PARTIAL
The Community Foundation for Greater New Haven  •  Up to $5,000 (budgets ≤$50k) or up to $10,000 (budgets >$50k), plus capacity-building support; typical award $3,000–$7,500  •  Round 2 opens Aug 3, 2026 (first-come until funds exhausted); Round 1 runs Jan 5–Feb 25 annually
Unrestricted small grants and general operating support for small community nonprofits. Round 1 has closed; Round 2 opens August 3, 2026 on a rolling first-come basis until funding runs out.

Environmental Justice Thriving Communities Grantmaking Program (Subgrants) 💵 CASH  PARTIAL
U.S. EPA (via 11 regional Grantmakers)  •  Tier 1 assessment $150k; Tier 2 planning $250k; Tier 3 project development $350k; plus $75k noncompetitive for capacity-constrained CBOs  •  Rolling — several regions (e.g., Region 2 Fordham, Region 10 Philanthropy NW) accept applications on a rolling basis through ~Nov 2026; awards every 3–4 months
Federal pass-through subgrants designed specifically to reach small, low-capacity grassroots groups that struggle with federal applications. A noncompetitive $75k Tier 1 track exists for capacity-constrained CBOs.
PARTIAL: Must be a nonprofit/CBO and apply through your EPA region's Grantmaker; the $75k noncompetitive Tier 1 is the lowest-effort entry. Confirm your region is still accepting before the Sept 30, 2026 award deadline.

Annual award funding nonprofits implementing innovative technology (incl. AI) projects with measurable community impact. The 2025 application window has closed; the program recurs annually with winners named at the September Tech Forward Conference.

MetisDAO Foundation  •  Explorer up to $20k, Voyager up to $50k, Navigator $50k+, Pioneer $100k+ (in METIS); Accelerate up to $25k  •  Rolling — applications reviewed continuously
Tiered, rolling ecosystem grants in METIS tokens for builders on the Metis Ethereum L2, including an Accelerate track for user-growth initiatives. Early-stage Explorer grants suit small teams with a working MVP.

UNDP-implemented GEF programme financing community-led environmental and sustainable-development projects. Grants go directly to grassroots CBOs/NGOs; each country runs its own call for proposals with local National Coordinators reviewing concept notes.

Funds grassroots organizations strengthening civic life and reducing polarization in rural America. In 2026 the program shifted from a closed nomination window to an open referral process — anyone can submit a referral, and selected groups are invited to apply.

Innovia Foundation — Community Grant Program 💵 CASH  PARTIAL
Innovia Foundation  •  $5,000 – $30,000 (one-year)  •  Next cycle expected early 2026 (last window Feb 16 – Apr 10, 2026)
Annual community grants of $5,000–$30,000 for nonprofit programs, projects, and capacity building across Eastern WA / North Idaho. The 2026 window (Feb–Apr) has closed; the cycle recurs annually.
PARTIAL: Small nonprofits eligible but region-locked to Eastern WA / N. Idaho; the 2026 round closed Apr 10, so watch for the early-2027 reopening.

Grants of roughly $75K–$115K plus technical assistance for community-based climate resilience projects. The 2026 application round closed March 12, 2026; the program recurs annually.

A quadratic-funding round pairing an ~$80K matching pool with community donations to fund Ethereum-aligned public-goods projects. Projects apply via Giveth and are matched based on number of unique contributors.

Fast, simple $8,500 unrestricted operating grants for rural Montana nonprofits promoting health, belonging, and leadership for kids and families. Decisions typically within two weeks; one award per org per 12 months.

Annual matching-fund grants for community-led digital equity work — expanding WiFi/connectivity, devices, and digital skills. Run jointly by Seattle IT's Digital Equity team and the Neighborhood Matching Fund.

Ben & Jerry's Foundation  •  Up to $30,000 (general operating)  •  Rolling Letter of Interest (by-invitation full proposal)
General-operating grants of up to $30,000 for small grassroots organizations using community organizing to advance social and environmental justice. Begins with a Letter of Interest; full applications are by invitation.

Costco Wholesale  •  Cash grants (sized to need; up to ~10% of program budget)  •  Rolling — accepted year-round (one application per Sep–Aug fiscal year)
Costco accepts monetary funding requests on a rolling basis throughout the year, regardless of distance from a Costco location. Decisions are based on community need and program type; only cash requests are considered (no in-kind).

Vermont Community Foundation  •  Up to $5,000  •  Rolling, Feb 23 – Nov 1, 2026
Funds consultant fees for organizational capacity-building work (fundraising, strategic planning, board development, tech). Applications accepted on a rolling basis Feb 23–Nov 1, 2026, reviewed continuously until funds run out.

Blue Mountain Center / Margolis & Company  •  $10,000 prize + one-month Blue Mountain Center residency (finalists $1,000)  •  July 1, 2026
Annual award for a developing nonfiction writer doing social-justice journalism. Applications accepted year-round but must be received by July 1; winner announced in November with the residency the following summer/fall.

Center for Environmental Journalism, University of Colorado Boulder  •  $80,000 stipend (9-month fellowship) + paid conference travel & reporting support  •  Next cycle: applications open December 2026, due March 1, 2027
Five fellows spend an academic year (Fall 2027–Spring 2028 cohort) at CU Boulder auditing courses, in seminars, and on a major project, with an $80,000 stipend. The 2026 cycle has closed; the next opens December 2026 with a firm March 1, 2027 deadline.

Forge Project  •  $25,000 per fellow  •  Next cycle expected early 2027 (2026 cycle closed Feb 15, 2026)

Superteam / Solana Foundation ecosystem  •  Microgrants ~$200–$10,000 (milestone-based)  •  Rolling — reviewed continuously
Aggregated grants portal listing live Solana ecosystem grants (incl. Solana Foundation USA Grants), reviewed on a rolling basis with fast microgrant decisions. Applicants must show how the project is a public good or fits a defined vertical.

Ragdale Residency Fellowships 💵 CASH  PARTIAL
Ragdale Foundation  •  Fee-waived 18-day residency + stipend of at least $1,000 (fellowship awards)  •  Next cycle: portal reopens February 2027 (2027-program deadline was May 14, 2026)
Ragdale awards ~150 residencies and ~12 fellowships a year; fellowship recipients get a fee-waived 18-day residency plus a stipend of $1,000+. The Submittable portal opens each February for the following year's sessions, with notifications by September.

The Toyota Foundation (Japan)  •  Up to JPY 5,000,000 (1-year) or JPY 10,000,000 (2-year) projects — roughly $33K–$65K USD  •  Annual cycle — 2026 window closed May 30, 2026; next call expected ~April 2027
