WBDC Accelerate Grant 💵 CASH  PARTIAL
Women's Business Development Council (Connecticut)  •  Up to ~$10,000 (cash)  •  Next round opens June 15, 2026
Cash grants to CT women-owned businesses through WBDC's grant programs. The Accelerate round's next funding window opens June 15, 2026; Ignite and Waterbury tracks recur on quarterly cycles.
PARTIAL: Geographically limited to Connecticut and to for-profit women-owned businesses, but low-effort and opens imminently (June 15). Only relevant if your org/team qualifies as CT women-owned.
women-owned · small business · Connecticut · cash   Open program →
Norfolk Southern — Thriving Communities & Safety-First Grants 💵 CASH  PARTIAL
Norfolk Southern Foundation  •  Varies (community grants)  •  August 3, 2026
Corporate community grants supporting thriving-communities and safety-first initiatives for nonprofits near Norfolk Southern's rail network. Fixed application deadline of August 3, 2026.
PARTIAL: Geographic eligibility (near a NS rail line in the eastern U.S.) is the gate; if your community qualifies it's a straightforward corporate application. Verify your location against their route map first.
community · corporate · place-based   Open program →
Growing Roots Grant 💵 CASH  PARTIAL
Roots (Powered by Roots)  •  Up to $10,000 per group + mentorship  •  Annual; 2026 round closed Feb 28 — next window expected early 2027
Micro-grants for grassroots youth-led climate-justice projects focused on narrative-building, advocacy, and community autonomy. The 2026 cycle closed Feb 28, 2026; the program runs annually, so the next call is expected in early 2027.
PARTIAL: Simple, low-effort micro-grant but the current window has closed — track it now and apply when the early-2027 call opens. Best fit for a young, frontline-community-led team.
climate justice · youth · Global South · micro-grant   Open program →
IKI Small Grants — 8th Call (expected) 💵 CASH  PARTIAL
International Climate Initiative (IKI), German Federal Government  •  EUR 60,000 – 200,000  •  Recurring; 7th call closed Mar 2026 — 8th call expected late 2026/2027
Global funding-plus-capacity program for local climate and biodiversity action. The 7th call's submission window closed March 17, 2026; calls recur, so the next (8th) call is anticipated in the following cycle.
PARTIAL: Substantial cash but a structured two-stage RFP and ODA-country/revenue eligibility limits. Watch the IKI Small Grants page for the next call announcement and prepare an eligibility registration early.
climate · biodiversity · Global South · capacity building   Open program →
Community Foundation for Palm Beach and Martin Counties — Competitive Grantmaking 💵 CASH  PARTIAL
Community Foundation for Palm Beach and Martin Counties  •  Up to $100,000 (program or general operations)  •  Annual; next cycle launches ~Nov 1, 2026
Competitive grants for general operations or programs, including a dedicated capacity-building priority (strategic planning, executive coaching, communications). The 2026 cycle closed Dec 2025; the next competitive cycle is expected to launch around Nov 1, 2026.
PARTIAL: Includes general-operating and capacity-building support (rare and valuable), but limited to Palm Beach/Martin County, FL nonprofits and competitive. Watch for the ~Nov 2026 cycle launch.
capacity building · general operating · Florida · community foundation   Open program →
Arbor Rising Grant 2026 💵 CASH  PARTIAL
Arbor Rising  •  $125,000 unrestricted (year 1) + 200–300 hours capacity-building consulting; renewable up to 3 years  •  Letter of Inquiry due June 9, 2026 (5pm ET)
Unrestricted multi-year funding plus hands-on consulting for promising mid-stage nonprofits addressing root causes of poverty. Four-stage process begins with a short LOI.
PARTIAL: Strong fit for a mission-driven small nonprofit but needs 501(c)(3) and is competitive/relationship-heavy. Act now — LOI closes June 9; submit the JotForm LOI immediately if eligible.
nonprofit · capacity-building · economic-mobility · unrestricted · poverty   Open program →
RISE Together Fund — 2026 Small Grants Fund 💵 CASH  PARTIAL
RISE Together Fund (an initiative of Proteus Fund)  •  Up to $25,000 (historical average ~$20,000); ~$700,000 total pool  •  Inquiries open May 11 – June 15, 2026 (5pm PT)
Open-call small grants for grassroots groups building community power and defending civil and human rights (speech, protest, religion), countering surveillance, xenophobia and Islamophobia. Low-burden inquiry form to start.
PARTIAL: Open to fiscally sponsored projects (no own 501(c)(3) required), so accessible to small groups — but mission must fit BAMEMSA community-power focus. Submit inquiry before June 15.
civil-rights · community-organizing · BAMEMSA · fiscal-sponsor-ok · grassroots   Open program →
California Advanced Services Fund — Broadband Adoption Account 💵 CASH  PARTIAL
California Public Utilities Commission (CPUC)  •  Grants for broadband adoption/digital-literacy projects (project-based; varies)  •  Recurring cycles closing January 1 and July 1 annually — next deadline July 1, 2026
State funding for digital-equity projects — digital-literacy classes, navigator services, devices and technical support — reviewed twice yearly until funds are exhausted.
PARTIAL: Open to CBOs/nonprofits but California-only and a government RFP with reporting requirements. If you operate in CA, prepare for the July 1, 2026 cycle.
digital-equity · broadband · california · nonprofit · civic-tech   Open program →
Tracy Family Foundation — Capacity Building Grant 💵 CASH  PARTIAL
Tracy Family Foundation  •  Up to $5,000 (requires ≥10% cash match from applicant)  •  2026 application opening soon — rolling review; confirm date on site
Small capacity-building grants for strategic planning, board development, leadership training and similar organizational-effectiveness work. Low-burden application aimed at small local organizations.
PARTIAL: Easy, small-dollar capacity grant for tiny orgs, but geographically restricted to west-central Illinois (or prior grantees) and requires a 10% cash match. Check the site for the 2026 opening date.
capacity-building · small-nonprofit · illinois · leadership · low-effort   Open program →
Verizon Small Business Digital Ready Grant 💵 CASH  LIMITED
Verizon (in partnership with LISC)  •  $10,000  •  Rolling — monthly reviews June–December 2026
Verizon and LISC award ten $10,000 grants per month from June through December 2026. Apply once and remain eligible for all remaining monthly draws through January 2027.
LIMITED: For-profit small businesses only — nonprofits ineligible. Listed because it's near-zero-effort cash for the individual/small-team side of the audience.
small business · cash · low-effort · rolling · LISC   Open program →
Whiting Creative Nonfiction Grant (Works-in-Progress) 💵 CASH  LIMITED
Whiting Foundation  •  $40,000 each to ten writers  •  Next cycle expected spring 2027 (2026 deadline was May 31)
Ten annual $40,000 grants to help nonfiction authors complete multiyear book projects. The 2026 round closed May 31; the program runs reliably each year with a spring deadline.
LIMITED: Individuals only (contracted nonfiction authors), competitive. Track for the spring 2027 cycle; not actionable for an organization.
writers · nonfiction · book · individual · stipend   Open program →
Adolph & Esther Gottlieb Emergency Grant 💵 CASH  LIMITED
Adolph & Esther Gottlieb Foundation  •  Up to $15,000 (typical award ~$5,000)  •  Rolling — no deadline, applications open continuously
One-time emergency cash assistance for established visual artists facing an unexpected catastrophe. No deadline; reviews take 4–6 weeks (24–48 hours in the most urgent cases).
LIMITED: Individuals only (established visual artists in genuine emergency), not orgs. Included as a low-effort, always-open cash option for member artists. Action: apply anytime documentation of the emergency is ready.
artist · emergency · visual arts · individual · rolling   Open program →
GovAI DC Summer Fellowship 💵 CASH  LIMITED
Centre for the Governance of AI (GovAI)  •  $21,000 stipend + travel support to Washington, DC  •  Annual; 2026 deadline (March 1) passed — next cohort expected early 2027
Three-month in-person Washington, DC fellowship (June–August) to launch or accelerate careers in AI governance and policy, with stipend and office workspace.
LIMITED: Individuals only (career fellowship), not org funding. Included as a stipended AI-governance opportunity for team members. Action: watch governance.ai for the early-2027 cohort call.
AI policy · fellowship · governance · stipend · individual   Open program →
Jobber Grants 2026 💵 CASH  LIMITED
Jobber  •  $10,000–$100,000 (9 grants, ~$250,000 total)  •  June 11, 2026 (Phase 1)
Awards up to $250,000 in 2026 to home-service/trades businesses (landscaping, HVAC, plumbing, cleaning, etc.), including a $100,000 grand prize. Flexible cash for growth, equipment, or staffing.
LIMITED: For-profit home-service businesses only — not nonprofits. Worth flagging to any allied trades small business in your network; Phase 1 closes June 11, 2026.
small-business · trades · cash · north-america   Open program →
Artist Grant 💵 CASH  LIMITED
Artist Grant (artistgrant.org)  •  $1,200 unrestricted  •  July 15, 2026 (awarded three times per year)
Small unrestricted cash grant to one visual artist per cycle, judged on artistic merit with no project requirement. Recurs three times a year; next deadline July 15, 2026.
LIMITED: Individuals only (not orgs) and carries a $30 fee. Useful for an affiliated individual artist; very low effort and a firm recurring deadline.
artist · visual-art · unrestricted · individual   Open program →
TechCrunch Startup Battlefield 2026 💵 CASH  LIMITED
TechCrunch (Yahoo)  •  $100,000 equity-free prize (Top 20 cohort benefits for all selected)  •  June 8, 2026 (extended)
Flagship startup competition awarding a $100,000 equity-free prize plus investor access and global visibility. Free application; Top 20 pitch live at Disrupt.
LIMITED: For-profit startups only — not open to nonprofits/community groups. Listed because the $100k is non-dilutive cash and the application is free; relevant only if a small commercial team is spun out. Deadline closes June 8, 2026.
startup · pitch · equity-free · disrupt · competition   Open program →
Reuters Institute Journalist Fellowship Programme 💵 CASH  LIMITED
Reuters Institute for the Study of Journalism, University of Oxford  •  £2,000/month stipend + fees, travel, and visa costs covered  •  Applications reopen January 2027 (2026–27 round closed Feb 13, 2026)
A fully funded fellowship at Oxford with a £2,000/month stipend for fellowships starting Oct 2026 / Jan or Apr 2027. The 2026–27 cycle is closed; applications reopen in January 2027.
LIMITED: For individual journalists, not organizations — falls outside the small-org/nonprofit frame, but flagged as real non-dilutive cash for an eligible team member. Reopens Jan 2027.
journalism · fellowship · stipend · research · media   Open program →
Mila AI Policy Fellowship 💵 CASH  LIMITED
Mila – Quebec AI Institute  •  Paid (hourly rate within a set range based on experience, ~15 hrs/week); travel and event costs covered  •  2026 cohort closed April 16, 2026; next annual cohort expected early 2027
Six-month paid fellowship bringing policy, social-science, and AI experts together to work on real-world AI governance challenges at a leading AI research institute.
LIMITED: Individuals only (not orgs) and the 2026 window has passed. On the radar as a recurring annual fellowship — set a reminder for the early-2027 cohort. Good for a staff member doing AI-policy work.
AI policy · fellowship · governance · stipend   Open program →
Siegel Public Interest Technology Summer Fellowship (PIT-SF) 💵 CASH  LIMITED
Princeton University — Center for Information Technology Policy (CITP)  •  Paid summer fellowship (stipend)  •  2026 cycle closed; next application cycle opens Fall 2026
Funded summer fellowship placing technologists in public-interest technology projects, hosted by Princeton's CITP. A pipeline opportunity for individuals on a small org's team.
LIMITED: Individuals only (not org-eligible); 2026 cycle closed. Recurring — applications reopen Fall 2026. Useful to flag to a technologist affiliated with the org rather than as org funding.
public interest technology · fellowship · civic tech · stipend   Open program →
Amex Shop Small Grants Program 💵 CASH  LIMITED
Main Street America & American Express  •  $20,000 grants (250 awards; $5M initial pool)  •  2025–2026 round closed Jan 16, 2026; recurring — next window expected ~Nov 2026 (Small Business Saturday)
Cash grants of $20,000 for physical-storefront small businesses to fund ready-to-execute community-impact projects, administered by Main Street America. Recipients have 6 months to complete projects.
LIMITED: For-profit small businesses only — not for nonprofits. On the radar only if the community includes a brick-and-mortar enterprise; recurring around Small Business Saturday, so watch for the late-2026 cycle.
small business · main street · storefront · corporate grant   Open program →
Spector Craft Prize for Emerging Artists 💵 CASH  LIMITED
Spector Family Foundation  •  $10,000 unrestricted to each of up to 5 artists, plus paid travel, summit participation and mentorship  •  2026 cycle closed March 1, 2026; next call opens November 2026
Annual unrestricted award for emerging American craft artists, with mentorship and a craft summit. Applications reopen each November.
LIMITED: For individual artists, not organizations — only relevant if a community member personally applies. Set a reminder for the November 2026 reopening.
craft · emerging-artist · unrestricted · prize · creative   Open program →
ITU AI for Good — Innovate for Impact ◐ CASH + CREDITS  PARTIAL
International Telecommunication Union (ITU)  •  Mentoring, prizes and resources (prize list announced May 2026); travel funding for Scholars from developing countries  •  2026 use-case/Scholar window closed; next call expected for the 2026–2027 cycle
ITU's AI for Good track that selects high-impact AI use cases and Scholars, offering mentoring, prizes, resources, and funded participation in the AI for Good Global Summit in Geneva (July 7–10, 2026).
PARTIAL: Mission-driven orgs (especially in developing countries) can submit AI use cases; benefits are mixed (mentoring, prizes, travel) rather than a large grant. Watch the page for the next 2026–2027 call.
AI-for-good · UN · use-cases · scholars · global   Open program →
Canon Oceania Grants Program 2026 ◐ CASH + CREDITS  PARTIAL
Canon Oceania  •  $5,000 per winner ($2,500 cash + $2,500 Canon product) across Community, Education, Environment & Story categories; plus 20th-anniversary studio packages  •  June 14, 2026 (11:59pm AEST/NZT)
Annual Canon Oceania grants give cash plus camera/print gear to community, education and environmental projects. Simple online entry; public votes on finalists in July, winners announced August 2026.
PARTIAL: Open to small community orgs and individuals, but restricted to Australia/New Zealand. If you operate (or partner) in AU/NZ, this is a low-effort form worth applying to before June 14.
community · creative · environment · education · AU/NZ   Open program →
IBM Impact Accelerator — AI for Education & Workforce Development ◐ CASH + CREDITS  PARTIAL
IBM (Corporate Social Responsibility)  •  Multi-year engagement with IBM technology + consulting (part of IBM's ~$45M AI accelerator commitment)  •  Last cohort RFP closed March 25, 2026; next global RFP expected ~early 2027
Annual global RFP pairing nonprofits with IBM technology and consulting to build AI solutions for education and workforce development. Recurring cohort program.
PARTIAL: Open to nonprofits/government globally and mission-aligned, but the 2026 RFP has closed and it's a competitive accelerator rather than direct cash. Watch IBM newsroom for the next cohort RFP (~early 2027) and prepare now.
AI-for-good · workforce · education · nonprofit · global   Open program →
Shophand $5k Small Business Boost Grant ◐ CASH + CREDITS  LIMITED
Shophand  •  $5,000 ($2,500 unrestricted cash + $2,500 services)  •  June 30, 2026
Quarterly micro-grant giving $2,500 unrestricted cash plus $2,500 of Shophand technical support to help a U.S. small business solve a tech/operations problem. Quick application.
LIMITED: For-profit small businesses only and half the value is in-kind services. Very low-effort; useful to pass to small-business partners. Next cutoff June 30, 2026, then recurs ~quarterly.
small-business · micro-grant · cash · tech   Open program →
☁ Credits & Compute — for later
Useful, but not real money. 16 new.
Alibaba Cloud AI Catalyst Program — Up to $120,000 lifetime cloud credits + up to 2B free Model Studio tokens
Spreadsheet Freedom Fund — 1-year DonorDock Lite CRM membership (in-kind, ~$1k+/org; ~$50k total across cohort)
Lambda Research Grant — Up to $5,000 in GPU cloud credits (B200/H100/A100)
Fireworks for Startups — ~$5,000–$10,000 in inference credits (plus higher rate limits)
Baseten AI Startup Program — Inference/compute credits + dedicated support (amount varies)
OpenAI Grove — $50,000 in OpenAI API credits + early model access + mentorship (5-week program)
Runway Builders Program — 500,000 API credits + early access (companion $10M fund writes equity checks up to $500K separately)
Nebius Research Grants Program — Free GPU compute credits / cluster access (academic year allocation)
NVIDIA Academic Grant Program — Up to 30,000 H100 GPU-hours, or up to 8 RTX PRO 6000 GPUs, or up to 2 DGX Spark systems
IBM Sustainability Accelerator — Two-year engagement with IBM technology + IBM Consulting / pro bono support (in-kind)
OpenAI for Startups — ~$2,500 (Ramp/partner) up to $5,000 (VC partner) in OpenAI API credits
Anthropic External Researcher Access Program — Free Claude API credits (standard model suite)
OpenAI Researcher Access Program — Up to $1,000 in OpenAI API credits (12-month validity)
ArbiFuel — Gas-fee sponsorship (ARB) covering user transaction costs
Foundation for Civic AI (FCAI) Grants — NVIDIA GPU compute cycles + cloud storage (in-kind, scaled to project need)
Mistral AI Startup Program (Mistralship) — Up to ~$30,000 in API credits (plus free rate-limited experiment tier for individuals)
Tracking 649 programs across teams, creators & orgs.
